sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Block at rows 10-14, cols 34-37 (top chamber)
# Check what's to the LEFT in the top chamber
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Top chamber (row 10, cols 10-55) ===")
print(f"  Row 10: '{ascii_lines[10][10:55]}'")
print(f"  Row 11: '{ascii_lines[11][10:55]}'")
print(f"  Row 12: '{ascii_lines[12][10:55]}'")
print(f"  Row 13: '{ascii_lines[13][10:55]}'")
print(f"  Row 14: '{ascii_lines[14][10:55]}'")

# Check yellow square position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        print(f"  Yellow: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'w' and n['pixels'] <= 10:
        print(f"  White cross: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Try moving LEFT to align with yellow square (cols 15-17)
# Block is at cols 34-37, need to move LEFT to cols 15-18
# Each LEFT moves 2 columns, so need (34-15)/2 ≈ 10 LEFT actions
# But let's first check if the path is clear
print("\n=== Path LEFT (row 10, cols 10-35) ===")
for i in range(10, 15):
    segment = ascii_lines[i][10:35]
    print(f"  Row {i:2d}: '{segment}'")

# Try moving LEFT several times
result = action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
print(f"\nAfter LEFTx5: done={last_action_result.get('done')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"  Orange: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
